original_hypothesis.title: BribeFactoryV3 Admin Recovery Calls Wrong Function, Causing Insolvency
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The recoverERC20AndUpdateData function in BribeFactoryV3 is intended to allow admins to recover tokens while updating the Bribe contract's internal accounting. However, it mistakenly calls IBribe.emergencyRecoverERC20 instead of IBribe.recoverERC20AndUpdateData. This removes the tokens from the Bribe contract without reducing the tokenRewardsPerEpoch mapping. When users subsequently try to claim their earned rewards, the Bribe contract will not have enough balance, causing claims to revert.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: While this requires admin action, it is a critical logic error in an operational function that directly leads to protocol insolvency and locked user funds.
preconditions: Admin calls recoverERC20AndUpdateData to manage bribe tokens

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: Calling emergency function instead of accounting-aware function
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Admin action inadvertently breaks contract solvency
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: No profit, causes DoS on user reward claims

economic_questions: How often is this recovery function expected to be used?
validation_requirements: Verify the function call mapping in BribeFactoryV3
Test user claims after recovery is executed

market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Breaks trust in the bribe system as users cannot claim rewards

potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: All remaining bribe tokens in the affected contract

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
